column1: I'm tired and can't help but be sleepy.

column1: That smell is wonderful, absolutely delightful.

column1: The child's crying is loud and unbearable.

column1: I can't help but watch that movie.

column1: He's not just excellent; he's perfect.

column1: Far from helping, he's getting in the way.

column1: In times like these, we can't afford to play around.

column1: Even the children laughed.

column1: There are even problems that teachers don't know.

column1: Not only work but also private life is fulfilling.

column1: Not only effort but also talent is necessary.

column1: I'm so frustrated I can't speak.

column1: Work is tough and I desperately want a break.

column1: The successful candidates are not just from this school but also from other universities.

column1: Today's test isn't just difficult; it's impossible.

column1: I was completely captivated by that scene.

column1: I was impressed by his talent.

column1: This job is too much; I can't handle it.
